sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports feeling comfortable since starting protonix and that his reflux is well-managed. He reports moving over from caffeine to green tea and feels much better. He denies any current symptoms, including chest pain, shortness of breath, or belly pain. He also denies nausea.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: The patient's blood pressure is within normal limits. His heart rate is 68 beats per minute. A slight 2/6 systolic ejection murmur is auscultated at the right upper sternal border. 1+ pitting edema is noted bilaterally in the lower extremities. The endoscopy revealed mild gastritis.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient has a history of reflux and gastritis. The examination reveals mild lower extremity edema, likely related to his history. The patient's symptoms are consistent with a resolution of his reflux.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Continue protonix 40 mg daily. Recommend lifestyle modifications including reducing caffeine, spicy foods, and alcohol. Follow up in 4 weeks to assess symptom control and edema management. Schedule a return visit if symptoms worsen or do not improve.
